title: Juvenile injured in Allston shooting
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A juvenile was injured in a shooting near 222 Brighton Ave in Allston, Boston, on Thursday evening.
The victim was found at 10 Allston St and taken to a hospital with non-life-threatening injuries.
Police are investigating; no arrests have been made as the shooter fled the scene.

title: He’s running his 11th Boston Marathon for Boston Children’s Hospital
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Steven Iannacone, 57, from Hingham, runs his 11th Boston Marathon supporting Boston Children's Hospital.
This marks a decade of running for Jace Perkins, born 1.6 pounds, now nearly 12 years old and thriving.
The relationship began when Iannacone met Jace's father through a mutual friend, inspiring him to run for Jace.

title: Did you follow the local news this week? Take our Greater Boston news quiz.
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: Boston.com released a news quiz to test knowledge on Greater Boston's latest trends.
Peter Chianca is the general assignment editor overseeing the quiz at Boston.com.
Chianca has been with Boston.com since 2019, with a background in editing and music writing.

title: Red Sox took advantage of MLB’s new automated strike challenge rules on Opening Day
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Red Sox utilized MLB's new ABS Challenge System effectively to secure a 3-0 win over the Reds on Opening Day.
Roman Anthony's successful ninth-inning challenge reversed a strikeout, prolonging the inning and sparking a two-run rally.
Boston started the season strongly, with Garrett Crochet and the bullpen combining for nine shutout innings.

title: In first Opening Day with Red Sox, Marcelo Mayer made quite the impression off the bench
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Marcelo Mayer had a strong Opening Day debut, going 2-for-2 and scoring twice in Red Sox's 3-0 win over the Reds.
Mayer, 23, was not initially in the starting lineup but performed well as a pinch-hitter in Cincinnati.
Manager Alex Cora emphasized Mayer's need to earn his roster spot, following his struggles with a .228 average last season.

title: Lucky pup thanks NH firefighters for quick thinking that saved his life
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Ziggy the dog was injured by a solar light while his owners were on vacation in New Hampshire.
Campton-Thornton firefighters applied a tourniquet, stabilizing Ziggy before he went to an animal hospital for surgery.
Quick actions by firefighters saved Ziggy's life, allowing him to recover from severe leg injuries successfully.

title: What’s causing my car to burn so much oil?
author: John Paul
bullets: A 2019 Subaru Legacy owner reports excessive oil consumption at 89,000 miles; Subaru suggests replacing the short block.
Subaru engines may experience oil control piston ring issues; an engine flush or PCV system check might help.
Replacing the short block is costly but could be a worthwhile investment given current vehicle prices and car condition.

title: This Nantucket diner just sold its fifth million dollar prize in the last 2 years
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Old South Diner in Nantucket sold its fifth winning lottery ticket over $1 million since March 2024, officials reported.
The latest $2 million winning Powerball ticket was sold at the diner on March 26, earning them a $20,000 bonus.
Previous major wins include a $1 million prize in March 2024 and a $2 million ticket on March 4, 2026.

title: School bus driver in crash that killed child was allegedly involved in hit-and-run months later
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Jean Charles, 39, was arraigned on involuntary manslaughter and vehicle homicide charges in the death of 5-year-old Lens Arthur Joseph.
Charles allegedly drove recklessly without conducting a safety inspection, leading to the fatal bus accident on April 28, 2025.
Charles faced further legal trouble with charges related to an August 2025 hit-and-run involving a suspended license.

title: 5 takeaways as Garrett Crochet, Marcelo Mayer steer Red Sox to 3-0 Opening Day win over Reds
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: Garrett Crochet led the Red Sox with six scoreless innings, striking out eight during the 3-0 win over the Reds.
Marcelo Mayer's pinch-hit double in the seventh inning was pivotal, helping the Red Sox take the lead.
Roman Anthony went 3 for 4, contributing to Boston's 12-hit effort in their season-opening victory.
